prompts: A neuron consists of a cell body, dendrites, and an axon. Signal conduction begins when a neuron is stimulated, creating an electrical impulse called an action potential. This impulse travels along the axon, away from the cell body. The axon terminals release neurotransmitters into the synapse, a small gap between neurons, transmitting the signal to the next neuron. The receiving neuron's dendrites pick up the neurotransmitters, converting the chemical signal back into an electrical impulse, continuing the process.
